objective: Implement the three foundational acoustic modules for Day 2: (1) types.ts â all shared interfaces and type aliases consumed across the acoustics module; (2) weighting-a.ts â ISO 61672-1:2013 A-weighting transfer function with zero hardcoded literals; (3) atmospheric-absorption.ts â ISO 9613-1:1993 atmospheric attenuation coefficient with full relaxation-frequency formulation. All files must satisfy RULE-001 through RULE-004 and compile under strict TypeScript.
phase_focus: Fase 4 â AcÃºstica Avanzada, Day 2 of 3

files_to_implement.description: Foundational TypeScript type definitions for the entire acoustics module. Defines all shared interfaces, enums, and type aliases consumed by weighting-a.ts, atmospheric-absorption.ts, spl-analyzer.ts, and future agents. Must be pure types â no runtime logic. String literal union WeightingType uses values that will be mirrored as exported constants in a future shared/ids.ts (RULE-002 stub comment included). All numeric ranges annotated with ISO/IEC standard references in JSDoc.

Implements the ISO 61672-1:2013 A-weighting transfer function. Computes the A-weighting correction in dB for any frequency using the exact pole-zero formula. All polynomial coefficients are derived at runtime from OCTAVE_BAND_FREQUENCIES and A_WEIGHTING_COEFFICIENTS imported from constants.ts â no hardcoded numeric literals. Exports a pure function computeAWeightingDb(frequencyHz) and a batch function applyAWeightingToBands(). Uses Math.PI and Math.pow â never hardcoded approximations (RULE-003).

Implements ISO 9613-1:1993 atmospheric sound absorption. Computes the attenuation coefficient Î± (dB/m) as a function of frequency, temperature, humidity, and pressure using the relaxation frequency formulas from ISO 9613-1 Â§6. All physical constants (reference pressure, reference temperature 293.15 K) imported from constants.ts. Uses Math.log10, Math.pow, Math.exp â no approximation literals (RULE-003, RULE-004). Exports computeAtmosphericAbsorption() and a batch variant for octave bands.

acceptance_criteria: types.ts exports at minimum: OctaveBand, WeightingType, SPLMeasurement, AtmosphericConditions, WeightingResult, FrequencyBand interfaces with full JSDoc
weighting-a.ts imports ONLY from constants.ts and types.ts â no hardcoded numeric literals for filter coefficients
atmospheric-absorption.ts uses Math.PI and Math.SQRT2 where applicable, imports ISO constants from constants.ts
All three files compile cleanly under tsconfig.json strict mode (tsc --noEmit passes)
No JSX syntax in any .ts file (RULE-001 compliance)
String identifiers for WeightingType enum values are exported from types.ts and re-exported via a future ids.ts stub comment (RULE-002 note)
Zero duplication of IEC/ISO numeric constants â all sourced from constants.ts (RULE-004)
vitest can discover and import all three modules without runtime errors
A-weighting transfer function H(f) matches ISO 61672-1:2013 Table 1 reference values within Â±0.1 dB at 1000 Hz (0.0 dB), 125 Hz (â16.1 dB), 8000 Hz (â1.1 dB)
Atmospheric absorption Î± at 1 kHz, 20Â°C, 70% RH matches ISO 9613-1 reference value of ~0.4 dB/100m within Â±5%
rules_applied: RULE-001: All files use .ts extension â pure TypeScript logic, no JSX syntax anywhere
RULE-002: WeightingType string literals documented with stub comment indicating future src/shared/ids.ts mirror â placed in files_pending
RULE-003: Math.PI, Math.log10, Math.pow, Math.exp, Math.sqrt used exclusively â no hardcoded decimal approximations for Ï, e, or physics constants
RULE-004: All IEC/ISO numeric constants (pole frequencies, reference pressure, reference temperature) imported exclusively from constants.ts â zero duplication
